title: delphinium altissimum subsp. drepanocentrum
intro: Delphinium altissimum subsp. drepanocentrum, also known as the meadow delphinium or larkspur, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, diuretic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Delphinium altissimum subsp. drepanocentrum include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
